rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN ROBINSON VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Defendant Ssw, Inc.S Costs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 18, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Plaintiffs¿ motion to strike or tax defendant SSW, Inc.¿s costs is granted in part and denied in part. The pendency of appeal is not a basis to defer ruling on a costs motion. Review of Exhibit C to the Plocki declaration shows $665 in allowable filing and motion fees. Review of Exhibit D to the Plocki declaration shows 1.2, not 2.2, hours spent by Mr. Frayne to summarize Mr. Brumley¿s deposition. This is not an allowable cost. The only evidence of the length of Mr. Brumley¿s deposition is paragraph 13 of Ms. Dickenson¿s declaration, which is 5.75, not 6.8 or 5.45, hours. Accordingly, 2.25 hours (1.2 for deposition summary and 1.05 for attending the deposition) of Mr. Frayne¿s time at $190 per hour, or $427.50, of the deposition costs is taxed and thus defendant is entitled to $4059.74 in deposition costs (4487.24 less 427.50). The Berry & Berry costs are in the nature of non-allowable attorney¿s fees and are therefore all $2379.97 sought for Berry & Berry¿s services is taxed. The RecordTrak expenses of $2152.17 are allowable in the court¿s discretion and the court so exercises its discretion since these costs appear to have been reasonably necessary. The Lexis/Nexis expenses of $266 are allowable in the court¿s discretion and the court so exercises its discretion since these costs appear to have been reasonably necessary. The expert costs of $4625.50 are disallowed in their entirety per CCP 1033.5(b)(1). Plaintiffs have withdrawn their request to tax any of the $263 in claimed service of process costs. In sum, defendant is entitled to $665 in filing and motion fees, $263 in service of process costs, $4059.74 in deposition costs, $2152.17 in RecordTrak expenses, $266 in Lexis/Nexis expenses for a total of $7405.91. = (220/HEK)
